mechanism: Weight-space pictures are only useful when the plotted directions are tied to the model perturbation being tested.
Low-barrier connectivity is the condition under which a direct average is plausible.
Same-basin finetunes can be averaged, but endpoint fallback and validation remain part of the method.
Permutation/gauge symmetry means hidden units or experts must be aligned before same-name averaging is trusted.
Parameter conflict should be measured and suppressed instead of blindly averaging every coordinate.
Layer/chunk heterogeneity and unlabeled hidden/logit alignment provide better merge coefficients than one global weight.
Expert contributions are cooperative/competitive, so weights should depend on marginal utility rather than a fixed average.
